title: Subtree Extraction — Move Directory to New Repo
description: The `packages/logger` directory has grown into its own concern and needs to be extracted into a new standalone repository `logger-repo` while preserving the full commit history for that directory only.
objective: A new repository must exist at `/workspace/logger-repo`. It must contain the contents of the `packages/logger` directory at the top level (not nested). It must contain only the commits that touched `packages/logger` — commits that didn't touch that path must not appear. The working tree of `logger-repo` must be clean.
hints: `git filter-repo --path packages/logger --path-rename packages/logger:` is the preferred way to extract a subdirectory with history. `git filter-branch --subdirectory-filter` is the legacy fallback.
Clone the repo first, then run the filter on the clone so the original repo is preserved.
After filtering, the contents of `packages/logger` should be at the root of the new repo.
